title: Ignore Rules, Attributes, Line Endings, and Repository Content · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Keep generated output, local configuration, binaries, and secrets out of history while preserving cross-platform text behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a content-boundary and normalization policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.
Choose repository ignore, info/exclude, and global excludes from whether a rule belongs to every collaborator or one environment.
Use attributes for text normalization, diff, merge, export, and binary behavior with check-attr evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Repository tracks config.local, build/app.bin, and CRLF script check.sh; .gitignore is added afterward. Decide tracked cleanup, attribute normalization, secret rotation, and changed-platform evidence. Competency probe: Trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.

Inspect the Ignore Precedence trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace ignore sources, directory scope, pattern grammar, negation, tracked-state limits, and check-ignore evidence.

content.text: For Ignore Precedence, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Ignore Precedence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Ignore Precedence record says a content-boundary and normalization policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Ignore Precedence without its stated preconditions, omit the a content-boundary and normalization policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Ignore Precedence for a content-boundary and normalization policy.
